title: Clinical and Environmental Pathogen Genomics: An Integrated Translational Workflow
abstract: This work describes a framework linking infectious disease clinical context with high-throughput sequencing and bioinformatics. We present reproducible quality control and analysis modules for viral, bacterial, and fungal genomic datasets and evaluate their utility in translational studies.

title: Comparative Performance of Shotgun Metagenomics and 16S Profiling in Complex Infectious Disease Cohorts
abstract: We compare shotgun metagenomic and 16S rRNA strategies for mixed microbial communities from clinically and environmentally relevant samples. The study highlights tradeoffs in taxonomic resolution, interpretability, and analytical reproducibility across sequencing and computational pipelines.

title: Reproducible Whole-Genome Sequencing Pipelines for Infectious Pathogen Surveillance
abstract: This publication outlines versioned WGS workflows including preprocessing, assembly, annotation, and variant analysis. We focus on transparent parameterization and cross-site reproducibility to support outbreak and surveillance research.
